mnemonics: Markov Chains Remember Only The Present: M.C.R.O.T.P. (A Markov Chain Remembers Only The Present state, not the full history)
keyPoints: A stochastic process is a collection of random variables representing the evolution of a system over time.
The Markov Property states that the future state depends only on the present state, not on the sequence of events that preceded it.
The joint probability distribution describes the probability of multiple random variables taking on specific values simultaneously.
commonMistakes: Assuming events in a stochastic process are always independent, when they are often dependent (e.g., in a Markov chain).
Confusing the Markov property (memoryless) with processes that have longer-term memory dependencies.
Miscalculating joint probabilities by not properly considering conditional dependencies between variables.
